perturbation.original_text:
9. TERMS AND TERMINATION
9.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for a period of 1 year after the Effective Date, unless earlier terminated as set forth herein (the "TERM"). This Agreement shall renew for successive 1-year periods, after the initial 1 Year Term, if agreed by both parties in writing within 30 days of license expiration. Either party may terminate the Agreement on 60-days written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment cease 30 days after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text:
9. TERMS AND TERMINATION
9.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue unless earlier terminated as set forth herein. This Agreement shall renew for successive periods, if agreed by both parties in writing. Either party may terminate the Agreement on written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment cease 30 days after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific duration 'for a period of 1 year after the Effective Date' and '60-days written notice', the modified text contradicts the idea of set terms. The removal of the duration and a period of notice creates ambiguity regarding how long the contract lasts and the period of notice required to terminate it, creating an internal contradiction within the same clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
9. TERMS AND TERMINATION
9.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue unless earlier terminated as set forth herein. This Agreement shall renew for successive periods, if agreed by both parties in writing. Either party may terminate the Agreement on written notice during a renewed term. However, in no event shall termination of this Agreement by MediaNet Group Technologies relieve it of the obligation to remit payment to Reseller for sales or Portals, Hosting Services or other products and services to or through Buyers contracted by Reseller prior to such termination. The obligation to remit payment cease 30 days after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 1-year term and 60-day written notice for termination. The changed text removes both of these, creating ambiguity regarding the contract's duration and termination notice period, and thus creating a direct contradiction to the original intent of clearly defined terms, and therefore an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text:
10. EFFECT OF TERMINATION
Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement:
10.1 Each party shall, within 30 days of such expiration or termination return to other party or destroy all Confidential Information and all other material received from such other party.
10.2 All rights granted by Reseller hereunder to MediaNet Group Technologies shall terminate. All rights granted by MediaNet Group Technologies hereunder to Reseller shall terminate, subject to the continuing obligation of MediaNet Group Technologies to remit payment pursuant to the provisions of Section 8.
10.3 Sections 5, 6, 7, 9.2 10, 11, 12 and 13 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text:
10. EFFECT OF TERMINATION
Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement:
10.1 Each party shall, within 30 days of such expiration or termination return to other party or destroy all Confidential Information and all other material received from such other party.
10.2 All rights granted by Reseller hereunder to MediaNet Group Technologies shall terminate. All rights granted by MediaNet Group Technologies hereunder to Reseller shall terminate.
10.3 Sections 5, 6, 7, 9.2 10, 11, 12 and 13 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the phrase 'subject to the continuing obligation of MediaNet Group Technologies to remit payment pursuant to the provisions of Section 8' creates a contradiction. Section 10.2 states all rights granted by MediaNet Group Technologies terminate, whereas Section 8 outlines payment obligations, implying those obligations should continue. Without the phrase, Section 10.2 now contradicts the ongoing obligation outlined in Section 8, leading to uncertainty about post-termination payment liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All rights granted by Reseller hereunder to MediaNet Group Technologies shall terminate. All rights granted by MediaNet Group Technologies hereunder to Reseller shall terminate.
perturbation.justification: The omission in Section 10.2 of the phrase 'subject to the continuing obligation of MediaNet Group Technologies to remit payment pursuant to the provisions of Section 8' directly contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty. The changed text implies that all rights and obligations of MediaNet Group Technologies terminate, conflicting with Section 8 which outlines specific payment obligations. Without the omitted phrase, Section 10.2 clashes with the ongoing obligations outlined in Section 8, causing a strong in-text contradiction regarding post-termination payment liabilities. The contradicted text, 'All rights granted by MediaNet Group Technologies hereunder to Reseller shall terminate,' is directly contradicted by the continued payment obligations specified in Section 8 because there are no longer subject to any caveats or continuing considerations; thus, Section 8 now has no authority.
